2605.18077.title: LLM-Guided Communication for Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
2605.18077.paper_summary: Communication is a key component in multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) for mitigating partial observability, yet prior approaches often rely on inefficient information exchange or fail to transmit sufficient state information. To address this, we propose LLM-driven Multi-Agent Communication (LMAC), which leverages an LLM's reasoning capability to design a communication protocol that enables all agents to reconstruct the underlying state as accurately and uniformly as possible. LMAC iteratively refines the protocol using an explicit state-awareness criterion, improving state recovery while narrowing differences in agents' knowledge. Experiments on diverse MARL benchmarks show that LMAC improves state reconstruction across agents and yields substantial performance gains over prior communication baselines.
2605.18077.paper_summary_zh: 溝通是多智能體強化學習（MARL）中減輕部分可觀察性的關鍵組成部分，但先前的方法往往依賴於低效的信息交換或未能傳遞足夠的狀態信息。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了基於LLM的多智能體通信（LMAC），它利用LLM的推理能力來設計一種通信協議，使所有智能體能夠儘可能準確且一致地重建潛在狀態。LMAC通過使用明確的狀態意識標準來迭代地完善該協議，提高狀態恢復的同時縮小智能體知識之間的差異。在多樣的MARL基準測試中的實驗表明，LMAC改善了智能體之間的狀態重建，並且在先前的通信基準上取得了顯著的性能提升。
2605.18077.author: Sangjun Bae et.al.
2605.18077.authors: Sangjun Bae, Yisak Park, Sanghyeon Lee, Seungyul Han
